config_files.notes: Primary configuration file containing graphics, monitor, and API settings. Edit only when the game is completely closed.
Alternate path for newer Steam installs where Star Trek Online is the root directory. Edit only when the game is completely closed.
Standalone launcher config location. Contains local settings overrides. Edit only when the game is completely closed.
Epic Games launcher config location. Contains local settings overrides. Edit only when the game is completely closed.

keys.notes: Fullscreen (1) allows the Cryptic Engine to acquire direct display focus, reducing input lag and potential DWM frame drop overhead.
Set manually to resolve issues where the game repeatedly opens on the wrong display.
Forces the game to render using DirectX 11. Legacy options like Direct3D9 are deprecated and can cause crashes or missing UI fonts on modern hardware.

keys.effect: Hard ceiling on rendering frame rate.
keys.notes: A value of 0 removes the ceiling (uncapped). On performance/balanced goals, capping the frame rate to match the monitor's native refresh rate prevents runaway GPU power utilization and thermal throttling, particularly in space combat and crowded social hubs.
Determines the default camera field of view. Higher values increase GPU rendering stress by bringing more scene objects into the camera frustum.

keys.effect: Field of view setting for ground missions.
keys.notes: Applies ground FOV separately. Recommended for motion-comfort modifiers to mitigate camera sickness during ground combat.

keys.effect: Field of view setting for space missions.
keys.notes: Applies space FOV separately. Higher space FOV improves spatial awareness during orbital dogfights.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Off or Low for performance; Medium for balanced; High or Max for quality.
manual_only_settings.reason: Shadow maps are heavily processed by the CPU and GPU in Star Trek Online, especially in dense fleet battles and social hubs (like ESD/Qo'noS). Disabling or lowering shadows provides the single largest framerate increase in the game.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Low/Medium for performance; High for balanced; Max for quality.
manual_only_settings.reason: Cryptic Engine lighting scales from basic vertex lighting up to advanced dynamic lighting. High/Max features deferred shading and bloom, which increase GPU render times.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Off for performance; MSAA 2x or FXAA for balanced; MSAA 4x for quality.
manual_only_settings.reason: MSAA reduces aliasing at the cost of VRAM bandwidth. FXAA is a post-processing filter that runs faster but slightly softens the entire image.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Low/Medium for performance; Medium/High for balanced; Max/Very High for quality.
manual_only_settings.reason: Space combat in STO features high densities of particle effects from abilities (e.g. Gravity Well, FAW). Lowering this setting prevents major frame drops when multiple abilities trigger simultaneously.

notes: Star Trek Online runs on the proprietary Cryptic Engine. Granular sub-settings like shadow mapping, lighting nodes, and anti-aliasing are not exposed directly as registry or easily-modified pref keys — they must be set via the in-game Options menu.

If your graphics settings fail to persist, ensure that gameprefs.pref is not set to Read-Only in its Windows file properties. If settings are corrupted, delete the file and relaunch the game to regenerate it.
The game is heavily CPU-bound in social zones (ESD, DS9, Qo'noS) due to rendering numerous other players. GPU-side settings will have lesser impacts in these hubs.
